change.behavior: For explicit detector current_location_relation ON/INSIDE, if current_location_id is not an existing node id but uniquely matches a same-frame observed object id or label, resolve it to that observed object id. Ambiguous aliases raise SpatialQAError instead of guessing.
change.evidence_scope: same-frame SceneObservation objects only; no QA gold answer, oracle required_edges, or evaluator-only fields are read.

expected_dsg_effect.memory_storage_content: preserves support/container current-location edges even when the detector uses a human label alias
expected_dsg_effect.query_content: GraphTool can now query explicit detector_current_location ON/INSIDE edges instead of falling back to IN_ROOM
expected_dsg_effect.query_order: explicit detector current-location remains ahead of inferred geometry and room fallback

next_experiment.required_external_return: detector/RGB-D JSONL may use stable object ids or unique same-frame support labels for current_location_id; ambiguous support labels must be disambiguated by the producer
next_experiment.rerun_needed: Rebuild detector-only observation sequence and predicted DSG after external detector/RGB-D return, then compare P33 DSG against VLM P26.

objective: increase DSG success by making external detector/RGB-D current-location memory easier to ingest without gold answers

problem.related_p32_failure_mode: relation degraded to IN_ROOM because support/container current-location evidence was not available as a graph edge
problem.root_cause: external producers may return a support/container label such as countertop instead of the stable graph object id countertop_1; before this change, ON/INSIDE current_location ingestion rejected that evidence with current_location_id node not found

green_commands: python -m pytest -q tests/test_spatial_qa.py -k 'support_fallback or current_location or support_like'
verification.red_test: python -m pytest -q tests/test_predicted_graph_builder.py -k current_location_label_alias failed before implementation with current_location_id node not found
